records.specific_details: Mercosur Trade Deal
records.intended_results: That the Government and MEPs must honour their commitments to stand up for farmers and defend their interests by opposing the current Mercosur trade deal.::To discuss farmers' concerns with the Minister for Foreign Affairs::To reiterate strong opposition to the Mercosur agreement in its current form. The beef industry in Ireland supports 90,000 farmers and around 25,000 jobs and is critical to the rural economy. There are very strong concerns about the impact the EU-Mercosur trade agreement would have on the viability of some 90,000 families.::That, while it is often argued that an additional 99,000t of beef from the Mercosur block countries would represent only 1-2% of the EU beef market, this statistic is in fact very misleading. The high value EU steak market (fillet, striploin, and cube) will be targeted by Mercosur beef exporters and will impact EU producers' ability to achieve a positive return on the carcass. 30% of the value of the carcass is contained within the yield of high

records.specific_details: Input into Housing Committee Work Programme
records.intended_results: Sought for the Housing Committee to prioritise sessions on implementing minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement and tenant protections against renovictions, and on scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes with multi-annual funding and a focus on worst-performing stock, to ensure climate targets are met while protecting tenants and reducing energy poverty.
lobbying_activities: Called for the Housing Committee to hold sessions on:Implementation of minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement, and tenant protections against renovictions.Scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes, with a focus on prioritising the worst-performing stock and shifting to multi-annual funding.Provided supporting evidence from ESRI analysis, Department consultations, and NGO research. - Email
Met with Deputy Hearne outlining the same - Meeting

records.specific_details: Letter to Oireachtas Housing Committee
records.intended_results: Influence Oireachtas Committee on amendments to social housing legislation related to appeal structures, access to homeless services, and entitlements of persons experiencing domestic violence.

records.specific_details: Bovine TB - Spring Lobbying 2025
records.intended_results: That the Minister must meet with IFA to agree on and implement a consistent and adequately resourced TB scheme, including dealing with infected wildlife, in all counties.::To highlight that this is an issue that requires urgent and meaningful actions to be brought back under control, but these actions must be well thought out, have a robust scientific basis and quantifiable in the contribution they will make to reducing TB levels.::That the measures and the entirety of the programme must have transparent KPI's that allow real time monitoring of effectiveness in reducing TB levels and where not contributing to these targets there must be flexibility within the programme to have them removed. All measures in the programme must refer to and identify the scientific studies that informed their inclusion in the programme.::That,  while there is an urgency to enhance the programme, it should not be at the expense of the time necessary to identify the most appropriate and effective measures th

records.specific_details: Nitrates Derogation - IFA Spring Lobbying 2025
records.intended_results: That the Government must redouble its efforts to lobby for the retention of the Derogation, honour its commitments on nutrient storage, and continue to focus on improving water quality from all sources.::To seek clarification on several critical matters. During a meeting with the Minister for Agriculture, Food, & the Marine, IFA were informed that the European Commission has requested the Irish Government to demonstrate more detailed compliance with the Habitats Directive as part of Ireland's application for the renewal of the nitrates derogation beyond 2025::To request that, in the interest of transparency, it is vital that the formal written communication from the Commission outlining this request is made available to stakeholders as a matter of urgency.::To demonstrate serious concerns that the requirement to prepare an Appropriate Assessment (AA) plan at catchment and/or sub-catchment level introduces significant additional legal and administrative burdens on Irish farmers. records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Mental Health Bill 2024 and concerns re inability to provide treatment to all who will be involuntarily hospitalised due to current iteration.To provide descriptions using video recordings of people who had benefited from treatment under the current Mental Health Act explaining their illnesses and the necessary treatment provided in a timely manner.
records.intended_results: To provide an understanding (for TDs) of being severely unwell with psychosis and severe depression from people with lived experience and how that manifested for them. To give lived experience insight into how necessary, and importantly helpful to recovery, it was to receive timely and urgent treatment as a patient under the current Mental Health Act and whom were not at immediate risk of harm or harming.

records.specific_details: Re-launch All Party Group on Rare Diseases. Update on the need for a new National Strategy for Rare Diseases, including background data on rare diseases in Ireland and the challenges that people living with rare diseases face.
records.intended_results: Understanding of the rare disease landscape and the priorities of people living with rare diseases.
lobbying_activities: 1 hour meeting of Oireachtas All Party Group on Rare Diseases meeting to mark Rare Disease Day in the AV Room of Leinster House - Meeting
Invitation to attend Oireachtas All Party Group on Rare Diseases meeting to mark Rare Disease Day - Email

records.specific_details: Youth Information Centre On line chat service
records.intended_results: Provide information on Youth Information chat service and circulate the 2024  Impact Report completed on the Youth Information Chat service which highlights the types and numbers of supports offered to young people on line.
lobbying_activities: Which included the 2024 Impact Report - Youth Information Chat Service - Email

records.specific_details: Accommodation Recognition Payment
records.intended_results: Extension of the Accommodation Recognition Payment (ARP) until March 2026 at its current rate

records.specific_details: Limerick Chamber Meeting with Deputy Wille O'Dea
records.intended_results: Limerick Chamber met with Willie O'Dea TD to discuss items critical to both industry and wider economic development. Ultimately this was boosting housing provision, increasing Gardaí numbers and crime reporting efficiency, and transport links
lobbying_activities: Email
Meeting

records.specific_details: Update on company operations in Ireland and EU
records.intended_results: To update policymakers/stakeholders on Lilly's investment in strategic manufacturing operations in Limerick and Kinsale; to highlight key policy areas of importance for Lilly's continued growth in both Ireland and across the EU, including advocating for EU legislative reform that ensures equal and timely access to innovations for all patients across Europe and the strengthening of legislative protections for intellectual property rights.
